title: Treasury Launches Cybersecurity Information Sharing Initiative for the Digital Asset Industry
summary: The Treasury Department's Office of Cybersecurity and Critical Infrastructure Protection (OCCIP) announced a new cybersecurity information-sharing initiative for eligible U.S. digital asset firms. The program extends access to the same cybersecurity threat information Treasury shares with traditional financial institutions, at no cost, to help firms identify, prevent, and respond to cyber threats.
key_points: New cybersecurity information sharing program for digital asset firms
Eligible firms can receive cybersecurity threat information from Treasury
Program advances President's Working Group recommendations on digital assets
Designed to strengthen defenses against growing cyber threats targeting digital asset platforms
macro_context: This initiative represents Treasury's first systematic effort to extend cybersecurity resources to the digital asset sector, potentially affecting how these markets operate and grow.

issues_found: event_type 'regulatory_guidance' is questionable. The source is a press release announcing an information-sharing program, not formal regulatory guidance. A more appropriate event_type might be 'research_paper' or a general category reflecting policy/program announcements.

The phrase 'extends cybersecurity resources to the digital asset sector for the first time' in the summary is interpretive rather than directly quoted. The source describes this as a 'new initiative' but does not explicitly use 'first time' language.

human_review.notes: The record is largely accurate and well-supported. The event_type issue is notable but not a factual error—the initiative could reasonably fall under broad policy/program categories. The source is current (April 9, 2026), has high domain trust (treasury.gov), and provides clear archival value as a new Treasury program affecting digital asset markets.

notes: This initiative represents Treasury's first systematic extension of cybersecurity resources to the digital asset sector. The program aims to strengthen defenses against growing cyber threats.
why_it_matters: This initiative advances Treasury's first systematic effort to extend cybersecurity resources to the digital asset sector, implementing recommendations from the President's Working Group on Digital Asset Markets. It reflects growing recognition that digital asset firms are an integral part of the U.S. financial system, and cybersecurity resilience is foundational to market stability and responsible innovation in this space.
